purpose: Adoption mode settings for the Mono & DIV Economy Game. Adoption mode changes how quickly demand and adoption can grow. It does not directly change difficulty.
principle: High adoption is not automatically easier. Faster adoption can create bubbles, liquidity stress, treasury inventory depletion, regulatory attention, and weaker adoption quality.

low.label: Low Demand
low.description: Slow public interest, slower trial use, weaker speculation, and less regulatory attention.
notes: Treasury inventory is usually easier to preserve.
Fiat displacement will be much slower.

normal.label: Normal Demand
normal.description: Balanced default adoption mode. Demand and adoption grow at a plausible base rate.
notes: Designed to work with Normal difficulty.
Growth can be good or dangerous depending on liquidity and treasury control.

high.label: High Demand
high.description: Faster attention, trial use, speculative demand, and adoption growth. More upside, but more stress.
notes: Can drain treasury coin inventory faster.

hyper.label: Hyper Adoption
hyper.description: Extreme attention and demand. This is a stress-test mode where adoption can outrun liquidity, treasury capacity, and regulatory tolerance.
notes: This mode is not automatically easy.
Mono may face support tests if adoption quality is weak.
Regulatory and banking friction should rise earlier.

lowQualityAdoption: Growth that depends on unsustainable dividend expectations

engineNotes.demandIsFlow: Demand is the amount of desired buying or selling during a period.
engineNotes.adoptionIsStock: Adoption is the accumulated base of users, merchants, businesses, and institutions.
engineNotes.attentionIsNotAdoption: A viral event can increase awareness without creating durable economic use.
engineNotes.fastGrowthCanBeFragile: Fast adoption should increase upside and stress at the same time.
